description: Advanced persistent threat persistence, stealth tunneling, credential abuse, and long-dwell tradecraft response playbook.

actions.action: Preserve volatile memory, persistence artifacts, Windows event logs, EDR telemetry, and authentication records before remediation.
Expand investigation scope to adjacent systems, privileged accounts, VPN access, jump hosts, and administrative sessions for lateral movement analysis.
Hunt for persistence mechanisms including scheduled tasks, services, Run keys, startup folders, WMI subscriptions, and port proxy configurations.
Review recently created or modified Windows services, unsigned binaries, and anomalous service accounts on affected assets.
Investigate FRP / reverse proxy tooling, SSH reverse tunnels, netsh portproxy usage, and encrypted outbound connections.
Review RDP activity, privileged logons, VPN authentication, and NTLM usage for potential credential abuse or unauthorized remote access.
Investigate NTDS.dit access attempts, credential dumping behavior, and suspicious use of ntdsutil, esentutl, or volume shadow copy tooling.
Review event log clearing activity, wevtutil usage, and telemetry gaps that may indicate defense evasion.
Search enterprise-wide for matching file hashes, process hashes, scheduled task references, and persistence binaries related to the investigation.
Consider temporary segmentation or containment of affected systems if active tunneling, persistence, or credential abuse is confirmed.
Prepare staged credential rotation for impacted administrative, service, and VPN accounts after forensic preservation is complete.
Perform historical telemetry review (30–90 days) to identify signs of long dwell time, beaconing, or repeated administrative access.
